images.caption: Figure 1:Left: OPD accelerates exploration but cannot exceed the model ceiling.Middle: a stronger teacher does not guarantee a better student.Right: advantage rises while length explodes or crashes.

Figure 3:Pass@kkperformance of Qwen3-1.7B-Base trained via different methods. OPD variants outperform Base and GRPO models in low-kk. However, the convergence at higherkksuggests OPD accelerates exploration rather than raising the model’s performance ceiling.

Figure 4:Weak teacher outperforms strong teacher in OPD w/ Clip.Benchmark accuracy (first two panels)
and informativenessℐ\mathcal{I}on the validation set for OPD w/ Clip runs distilling from four teachers with varying capabilities into the same Qwen3-1.7B-Base student.

Figure 5:Avg@32 across varying prompt-to-rollout configurations.Under a fixed batchBB, we vary the rollout countnnper
prompt. Maximizing problem diversity consistently outperforms a larger sampling size.

Figure 6:Length exploitation illustration. Advantage increases, while accuracy drops. Teacher→\rightarrowStudent: Qwen3-4B-GRPO→\rightarrowQwen3-1.7B (Mode A), and Qwen3-1.7B-GRPO→\rightarrowQwen3-1.7B-Base (Mode B).

Figure 7:(a)A correct rollout receives a mildly negative token-mean.(b)Mode A (Endless Exploration): a long wrong rollout with negative scores diluted across tokens, failing to penalize the incorrect response.(c)Mode B (Abrupt Degeneration): a short, fully teacher-preferred rollout prefix that yields a strongly positive token-mean despite an incorrect outcome.
Table 1:Performance of different methods on benchmarks. Among all evaluated downstream student models (bottom two blocks), the overall best scores areboldedand the second best areunderlined.
Figure 8:Hard Clipping collapses all tokens beyond the[cmin,cmax][c_{\min},c_{\max}]to the same value (ranking lost). Log-scale compression preserves ordering across the full range.

Figure 9:Training dynamics of OPD variants. Teacher: Qwen3-4B-GRPO; Student: Qwen3-1.7B-Base. Vanilla OPD suffers greater
length exploitation, whereas Clip producesshorter responses and achieves higher accuracythan naive OPD.
